name: Parser Combinator Library
description: Implement a small parser combinator library across two modules. Module parser_core.py must define: ParseResult dataclass with fields success (bool), value (Any), remaining (str), error (str, default ''); Parser class wrapping a function (str -> ParseResult) with methods: __call__(text), then(other) sequence (returns tuple (a, b)), or_else(other) tries first then second on failure, map(fn) transforms successful value, many() returns list of zero-or-more matches. Module combinators.py must import Parser from parser_core.py and provide module-level functions: char(c) parses a single specific character; digit() parses one digit char; literal(s) parses an exact substring; whitespace() consumes any number of spaces/tabs (including zero) returning the consumed string; integer() parses signed/unsigned integer and returns Python int.
test_imports: from combinators import char, digit, literal, whitespace, integer
signature:
# parser_core.py
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any, Callable

@dataclass
class ParseResult:
    success: bool
    value: Any
    remaining: str
    error: str = ''

class Parser:
    def __init__(self, fn: Callable[[str], ParseResult]): ...
    def __call__(self, text: str) -> ParseResult: ...
    def then(self, other: 'Parser') -> 'Parser': ...
    def or_else(self, other: 'Parser') -> 'Parser': ...
    def map(self, fn: Callable) -> 'Parser': ...
    def many(self) -> 'Parser': ...

# combinators.py
from parser_core import Parser, ParseResult

def char(c: str) -> Parser: ...
def digit() -> Parser: ...
def literal(s: str) -> Parser: ...
def whitespace() -> Parser: ...
def integer() -> Parser: ...
tests:
def test_digit_fail():
    assert digit()('xyz').success is False

def test_literal_fail():
    assert literal('hello')('helXo').success is False

def test_then():
    p = char('a').then(char('b'))
    r = p('abc')
    assert r.success and r.value == ('a', 'b') and r.remaining == 'c'

def test_or_else():
    p = char('a').or_else(char('b'))
    assert p('abc').value == 'a'
    assert p('bcd').value == 'b'
    assert p('xyz').success is False

def test_map():
    p = digit().map(int)
    r = p('5x')
    assert r.success and r.value == 5

def test_many():
    p = digit().many()
    r = p('123abc')
    assert r.success and r.value == ['1', '2', '3'] and r.remaining == 'abc'
constraints: Parser is composable: combinators return new Parser objects, do not mutate
many() must succeed even with zero matches (returns empty list)
Failed parsers must leave remaining text unchanged from where they started
whitespace() consumes spaces and tabs only and always succeeds
